danger_signals.visual_form: Klaxon-styled auditory alert; fuel gauge at bottom of screen depleting
danger_signals.meaning: Player is running out of fuel and will lose jet if not replenished soon

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual lane boundaries on left and right edges of screen
danger_signals.meaning: Collision with river bank will cause immediate jet loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Tankers, helicopters, and bridge structures appearing in scrolling river
danger_signals.meaning: Active combat threats that deal damage or cause collision if not destroyed

danger_signals.visual_form: Helicopter sprites with projectile animation (in Atari 8-bit version)
danger_signals.meaning: Escalated threat level; enemies are now actively aggressive rather than static obstacles

safety_signals.visual_form: Distinct fuel tank sprite positioned in the river corridor
safety_signals.meaning: Flying over refuels the jet; restores fuel gauge at bottom of screen

safety_signals.visual_form: Bridge structure spanning the river width
safety_signals.meaning: Successfully destroying a bridge marks a save point; player restarts at that location upon losing a life instead of earlier in the level

safety_signals.visual_form: Empty river sections with no enemy or obstacle sprites
safety_signals.meaning: Safe passage zone; allows fuel conservation and repositioning

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player jet positioned at bottom of screen; controlled by player input

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Helicopters in Atari 8-bit version fire projectiles; bridges may have tanks on them (bonus destruction points)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enemy structures and units scroll downscreen toward player; represent blockade positions along River of No Return

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fuel depots appear frequently in the river corridor; sparse enemy formations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can maintain fuel safely; enemies are stationary or predictable; difficulty is low

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fewer fuel depots; higher density of enemy tankers, helicopters, and bridges
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must make strategic fuel conservation choices; risk-reward between collecting points and surviving; helicopters fire back at higher difficulties

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fuel gauge depletes visibly at bottom of screen; Klaxon alert sound activates
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must prioritize fuel depot collection over combat or point scoring; heightened tension and urgency

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bridge sprite disappears from river; new section begins
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Upon jet loss, player restarts at destroyed bridge location rather than earlier; indicates forward progress saved

environmental_storytelling.example: Vertical scroll continuously moves player forward through the 'River of No Return,' establishing setting as a deep infiltration raid behind enemy lines with no retreat option
Tankers, fuel depots, and bridges represent enemy supply and defensive lines; destroying them communicates player role as saboteur dismantling enemy logistics
Fuel depots decrease in frequency as player progresses, visually indicating supply lines are being disrupted and player is deeper in hostile territory
Narrow corridor between left and right banks forces tactical positioning and creates sense of constrained movement through enemy-held territory

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Static; player must fly over it to collect fuel; can be destroyed for points but destroys fuel source
Static position in corridor; must be destroyed by player missiles; represents mobile enemy supply
Fires projectiles at player in advanced versions; indicates escalated threat level and enemy active defense
Spans full river width; blocking passage; destruction marks checkpoint; may carry tanks on surface (visual variant)
Klaxon warning activates; visual fuel gauge flashes or depletes; forces immediate fuel-seeking behavior

notes: River Raid communicates world-state primarily through resource scarcity (fuel depletion), checkpoint architecture (bridge destruction), and enemy density escalation. The game uses no explicit faction colors or symbols; instead, faction identity is implicit through role (player jet = ally, all obstacles = enemy forces). Visual communication relies on sprite density, fuel gauge status, and Klaxon audio cues rather than detailed graphical storytelling. The 'River of No Return' concept is communicated through continuous downscreen scrolling with no option to retreat, establishing the raid narrative without explicit narrative UI.
